tool_args.label: MTA 2022 Final Proposed Budget November Financial Plan 2022-2025 Volume 2
tool_args.raw_text: MTA 2022 Final Proposed Budget November Financial Plan 2022-2025 Volume 2 November 2021

payload.title: MTA 2022 Final Proposed Budget November Financial Plan 2022-2025 Volume 2
payload.description: Volume 2 of the MTA's November Financial Plan includes MTA-Consolidated detailed financial and position schedules as well as narratives supporting the baseline projections included in the 2022 Final Proposed Budget and the Financial Plan for 2022 through 2025. Also included are Agency sections with descriptions of Agency Programs and supporting baseline tables.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Volume 2 includes MTA-Consolidated detailed financial and position schedules as well as the narratives that support the baseline projections

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: MTA Bus Company
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Bus Company

payload.description: MTA Bus Company (MTA Bus) is an MTA agency. The November Plan projects MTA Bus will only reach 87.5% of pre-pandemic ridership by the start of 2024. 2022 Final Proposed Budget Total Expenses before Depreciation and OPEB of $929.5 million, Total Revenue projected at $204.6 million.

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA Bus Company (MTA Bus) continues to emerge from the COVID-19 pandemic.
The Plan predicts ridership to be 95.0 million in 2022; 103.0 million in 2023, and 104.8 million in 2024 and 104.5 million in 2025.
MTA Bus Company's 2022 Final Proposed Budget includes Total Expenses before Depreciation and Other Post-Employment Benefits of $929.5 million

tool_args.label: MTA Bus Company COVID cleaning cost reductions
tool_args.raw_text: Revised estimates of the cost of COVID-19 preventative measures are expected to result in a projected decrease in expense of $24.2 million in 2021, $16.8 million in 2022, and $15.4 million in 2023, $15.6 million in 2024 and $15.9 million in 2025. This is due to the change in bus sanitizing frequency from twice per day by hand to once per day using electrostatic sprayers, which reduce cleaning time.

payload.claim_text: Revised estimates of the cost of COVID-19 preventative measures are expected to result in a projected decrease in expense of $24.2 million in 2021, $16.8 million in 2022, and $15.4 million in 2023, $15.6 million in 2024 and $15.9 million in 2025. This is due to the change in bus sanitizing frequency from twice per day by hand to once per day using electrostatic sprayers, which reduce cleaning time.
payload.description: COVID-19 sanitizing cost reduction from hand to electrostatic spraying

evidence_refs.source_quote: Revised estimates of the cost of COVID-19 preventative measures are expected to result in a projected decrease in expense of $24.2 million in 2021, $16.8 million in 2022, and $15.4 million in 2023, $15.6 million in 2024 and $15.9 million in 2025. This is due to the change in bus sanitizing frequency from twice per day by hand to once per day using electrostatic sprayers, which reduce cleaning time.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer

tool_args.label: MTA Bus Company financial outlook fragile
tool_args.raw_text: Despite the improvement, the MTA Bus financial outlook remains fragile, with current projections based on the midpoint between the McKinsey best-case and worst-case scenarios forecasting MTA Bus will only reach 87.5% of pre-pandemic ridership by the start of 2024, resulting in significantly less farebox revenue. This large reduction in farebox revenue makes the agency's financial outlook precarious

payload.claim_text: Despite the improvement, the MTA Bus financial outlook remains fragile, with current projections based on the midpoint between the McKinsey best-case and worst-case scenarios forecasting MTA Bus will only reach 87.5% of pre-pandemic ridership by the start of 2024, resulting in significantly less farebox revenue. This large reduction in farebox revenue makes the agency's financial outlook precarious
